page_title: Hunter Association Official Issue: Hunter's Guide
text:
The Hunter × Hunter Hunter Association Official Issue: Hunter's Guide; Character & World Official Databook (ＨＵＮＴＥＲ × ＨＵＮＴＥＲハンター協会公式発行ハンターズ・ガイド; キャラクター＆ワールドオフィシャルデータブック, HUNTER×HUNTER Hantā Kyōkai Kōshiki Hakkō Hantāzu Gaido; Kyarakuta & Warudo Ofisharu Detabukku) is a databook published on June 4, 2004. It contains information on characters and aspects of the Hunter × Hunter world which may or may not be included in the manga and/or anime.

Pre-Index Contents

Pages 1 - 10: The guidebook's first ten pages contain illustrations.
The "Special Pin-Up" (スペシャルピンナップ, Supesharu Pinnappu) begins on page 1, as indicated by the index. It is a special two-sided fold-out page that illustrates Gon Freecss, Killua Zoldyck, Kurapika, and Leorio Paradinight on each side.
The Mini Illustration Collection (ミニイラスト集, Mini Irasuto Shū) begins on page 7, indicating that the previous "Special Pin-Up" fold-out "page" should be counted as six pages. Page 7 is an illustration of Gon. Pages 8 and 9 combine to show an illustration of Kurapika, Gon, Leorio, and Killua (from left to right). Page 10 is an illustration of Killua and Gon, with their full names spelled as "Killua Zaoldyeek" and "Gon Freecss." Note: Killua's full name is spelled as "Killua Zaoldyeck" on page 30 of the databook, as part of "Hunter New Face File 02".
Page 11: The eleventh page is the guidebook's title page.

Index

The databook's index starts on page 12 and ends on page 13. Pages appearing before the index are listed at the top of page 12, including the "Special Pin-Up" starting on page 1 and the "Mini Illustration Collection" starting on page 7.

Hunter Character 50 Sound Table

The "Hunter Character 50 Sound Table" (ハンター文字50音表, Hantā Moji 50 Otohyō) is a chart found on page 13, after the index. It displays the entire Hunter × Hunter Alphabet to help readers read Hunter Characters found throughout the databook.

Hunter New Face Files

The "Hunter New Face Files" (ハンターニューフェイスファイル, Hantā Nyūfeisu Fairu) section starts on page 14. Pages 14 and 15 contain an introduction to the New Face Files section, which begins with the introduction of Gon Freecss on page 16.

Challengers to the Unknown

The "Challengers to the Unknown" (未知への挑戦者たち, Michi e no Chōsenshatachi) section starts on page 62. Pages 62 and 63 serve as an introduction to the Challengers to the Unknown section. The first character to be introduced in the section is Ging Freecss starting on page 66.

Nen Ability Guide: Nen-troduction

The "Nen Ability Guide: Nen-troduction" (念能力の手引き: NEN-TRODUCTION, Nen Nōryoku no Tebiki: NEN-TRODUCTION) section starts on page 88. Pages 88 and 89 serve as an introduction to the Nen Ability Guide: Nen-troduction section. The section is dedicated to the explanation of Nen.

What are Nen abilities? (Page 90)
Nen Training (Page 92)
Nen System (Page 94)
Nen Practical Application (Page 98)

Criminal and Extraordinary Individual Files

The "Criminal and Extraordinary Individual Files" (犯罪者·異生物ファイル, Hanzaisha · Iseibutsu Fairu) section starts on page 100. Pages 100 and 101 serve as an introduction to the Criminal and Extraordinary Individual Files section. The first character to be introduced in the section is Hisoka Morow, starting on page 102.

People and the World: World Guide

The "People and the World: World Guide" (人と世界: ワールドガイド, Hito to Sekai : Wa-rudo Gaido) section starts on page 154. Pages 154 and 155 serve as an introduction to the People and the World: World Guide section. The first subsection, on the Hunter Exam Hall, starts on page 156.

Funter × Funter

The "Funter × Funter" (ファンたー×ファンたー, Fanta × Fanta) section starts on page 210. Pages 210 and 211 serve as an introduction to the Funter × Funter section. The first subsection is about the "Ranking Island," R.I. for short, starting on page 212.

Post-Index Contents

External Links

HUNTER×HUNTERハンター協会公式発行ハンターズ・ガイド／冨樫 義博 集英社の本 公式 (Hunter × Hunter Hunter Association Official Issue: Hunter's Guide/Yoshihiro Togashi official Shueisha's book) [Official website in Japanese]
